title: Robust Korean Shiitake Noodles
description: A fresh and crisp Korean dish featuring shiitake mushrooms, this recipe is a crowd-pleaser and flexitarian-friendly. It originates from the Jeju region of Korea.
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the noodles according to the package instructions.
In a large skillet, heat the sesame oil over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms to the skillet and cook until they are tender and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce and 1 tablespoon of water.
Pour the soy sauce mixture over the mushrooms and garlic in the skillet.
Add the cooked noodles to the skillet and toss to combine with the mushroom mixture.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the noodles to a serving platter and garnish with chopped green onions.
